They become so easy. Never forget this one fact. There's four levels of learning, right? You start off at the lowest level of learning when you are unconsciously incompetent. That means like you weren't even aware that tonality was an important function of communication. Like you were just unaware, flushing, then you learned a bit about the straight line perhaps and you said, oh my God, wow, there's this tonality stuff. It sounds really good, man. Wow, does that guy sound good? I don't know how to use it, but boy, I should probably learn that. So your awareness is heightened. That's called being consciously incompetent. From there, you said, I'm going to study the straight line. And here we are right now. You're in the process of becoming consciously competent. If you're not already so. In other words, you can use tonality now. You can apply these tonalities. You're getting great results, but I'm sure you are, because it works really fast. But you're not at that highest level of learning, which is being unconsciously competent, which means you can now do this stuff with your eyes closed. That's where you're going to get. And the only way you get this through practice, repetition, and drilling, and remaining in the right place and remember those shortcuts I gave you about breaking up into groups, having a partner, analyzing what someone else is saying, and then say, hey, when you want it to sound scarce, you were moving your body this way, and you were certain you moved your body that way. Break up into groups, trust me, it's huge. It's so important. And you really can get these little hacks or shortcuts into mustering tonalities that are giving you problems. All right?
